title: The Robot Who Counted Feelings
description: Bex the robot is trying to understand if a message is happy or sad — but feelings turn out to be trickier than numbers.

steps.content:
Bex was given one job: read messages and decide if they were Happy, Sad, or Neutral. Simple, right?

The first message was: 'I got ice cream today!' Bex spotted the word 'ice cream' — that was in the Happy column. Easy.

The second message was: 'I can't believe I missed the bus AGAIN.' Bex spotted 'can't' and 'missed' — both Sad words. Also easy.

Then came the third message: 'Oh great, another Monday.' Bex froze.

steps.question: 'Oh great, another Monday.' Is this message happy or sarcastic (actually unhappy)?
options: Sarcastic — the person probably means the opposite of what they said
Neutral — Monday is just a day

steps.content:
Bex had never learned about sarcasm. She only knew what words *usually* meant — not what people sometimes *mean* when they say them.

This is a real challenge for AI systems that read text. They can count positive and negative words very well. But human communication is layered. The same sentence can mean completely different things depending on tone, context, and who's saying it.

AI that reads feelings is called a *sentiment analyser* — and even the best ones get sarcasm wrong more often than you'd expect.

steps.instruction: Which of these sentences would be hardest for Bex to understand correctly?
options: 'This is the best day of my life' — said by someone who just spilled their lunch

steps.content:
Bex learned something important that day: understanding language isn't just about counting words. It's about knowing the whole story — who's speaking, what happened before, and even the tiny clues humans use like emojis, capital letters, and tone.

She asked her trainer for more examples. Not just words, but *context*. Stories. Conversations.

Months later, Bex got sarcasm right 80% of the time. Not perfect — but much better.

'80% is good!' said her trainer.

'Oh, *great*,' said Bex.

The trainer laughed. Bex had learned something else too.
